data.content:
The problem is clear. The target generates the file successfully, but never adds it to the `@(Compile)` item group, so Roslyn doesn't know to compile it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile Include>` item after writing the file:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but MSBuild's compilation step doesn't know about it because it wasn't included in the `@(Compile)` items.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item in the target after writing the file so that it gets included in compilation.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

 </Project>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. The issue was that the `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. Without that, MSBuild/Roslyn didn't include it in compilation. The fix adds:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

inside the target, so the generated file is picked up by the compiler.
